name: Cordel de Merinas Trail
name_original: El Cordel de Merinas – running circular desde Abelgas de Luna (Reserva de la Biosfera Valles de Omaña y Luna)
description: Jog the moderate 5.7-mile Cordel de Merinas Trail in Valles de Omaña y Luna Biosphere Reserve, featuring gorges, pastures, and scenic views.
region_intro: Jogging around Sena De Luna offers a diverse and naturally rich environment within the southern slopes of the Cantabrian Mountains, part of the Parque Natural de Babia y Luna. The region is characterized by mountain passes, gorges, and varied terrain, providing scenic routes for runners. From challenging inclines to accessible paths, the landscape includes forest views, mountainous areas, and the expansive Embalse de Barrios de Luna. Best jogging routes around Sena De Luna The most popular jogging route is Las Tres Marías Trail, a 7.0 miles (11.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 30 minutes to complete. This route features forest and mountain views within the Reserva de la Biosfera del Alto Bernesga. Another top favourite among local runners is Ruta de los Calderones Trail, a moderate 3.7 miles (6.0 km) path. This trail explores a geological and natural enclave, offering an engaging environment for trail jogging. Local runners also love the Cordel de Merinas Trail, a 5.7 miles (9.1 km) trail leading through historic drover's roads and scenic gorges, often completed in about 1 hour 5 minutes. Jogging around Sena De Luna is defined by mountain passes, gorges, and diverse terrain, including the Luna Reservoir. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more difficult ascents. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 40 runners have used komoot to explore Sena De Luna's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Sena de Luna?
A: There are over 10 dedicated running routes around Sena de Luna, offering a variety of experiences for different fitness levels. These trails are part of the diverse and naturally rich environment within the Parque Natural de Babia y Luna.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Sena de Luna?
A: The most pleasant times for outdoor activities, including running, in Sena de Luna are typically during &lt;em&gt;spring&lt;/em&gt; (April-June) and &lt;em&gt;autumn&lt;/em&gt; (September-October). During these seasons, the weather is mild, and the landscapes are vibrant, making for ideal running conditions.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails in Sena de Luna?
A: Runners in Sena de Luna can expect a diverse range of terrain. The region is characterized by challenging mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6333018" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cubilla Pass&lt;/a&gt;, scenic gorges such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4073598" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Calderones Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, and varied paths through forests and mountainous areas. You'll also find routes offering views of the expansive Embalse de Barrios de Luna (Luna Reservoir).

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running routes in the area?
A: While many routes in Sena de Luna are moderate to difficult, there are options suitable for a more relaxed jog. For example, the 'Sendero Sena de Luna - Pobladura' is a relatively easy circular route. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052018123/ruta-de-los-calderones-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de los Calderones Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 3.7-mile path that offers an engaging environment without extreme difficulty.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Sena de Luna?
A: Yes, Sena de Luna offers several circular running routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056008921/cordel-de-merinas-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cordel de Merinas Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a 5.7-mile loop that explores historic drover's roads and scenic gorges. The 'Sendero Sena de Luna - Pobladura' is also a relatively easy circular route.

Q: What interesting sights or landmarks can I see while running in Sena de Luna?
A: Many running routes in Sena de Luna offer stunning natural and historical sights. You might encounter the breathtaking &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4073598" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Calderones Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, or the unique sight of the submerged 'ghost town' in the Embalse de Barrios de Luna when water levels are low. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1055968994/las-tres-marias-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Tres Marías Trail&lt;/a&gt; offers beautiful forest and mountain views within the Reserva de la Biosfera del Alto Bernesga.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the running trails in Sena de Luna?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on trails in the region, including within the Parque Natural de Babia y Luna and the Reserva de la Biosfera del Alto Bernesga. However, it is always recommended to keep dogs on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all trail users. Specific regulations may apply in certain protected areas, so always check local signage.

Q: Are there any running routes suitable for families?
A: While some routes feature challenging inclines, families can find suitable options for a more relaxed jog. The 'Sendero Sena de Luna - Pobladura' is a relatively easy circular route that could be suitable for families. For a slightly longer but still manageable option, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052018123/ruta-de-los-calderones-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de los Calderones Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 3.7-mile path that explores an engaging natural enclave.

Q: Where can I find parking for the running routes?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. For popular routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1055968994/las-tres-marias-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Tres Marías Trail&lt;/a&gt; or the 'El Cordel de Merinas Loop from Abelgas de Luna' (which includes the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056008921/cordel-de-merinas-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cordel de Merinas Trail&lt;/a&gt;), you can typically find designated parking areas near the starting points in the respective villages or access roads. It's advisable to check specific route details for precise parking information.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about the trails in Sena de Luna?
A: The komoot community frequently praises Sena de Luna for its diverse and naturally rich environment. Runners particularly appreciate the stunning mountain and forest views, the varied terrain that caters to different fitness levels, and the opportunity to explore unique natural features like gorges and the Luna Reservoir. The well-maintained paths within the Parque Natural de Babia y Luna also contribute to a highly enjoyable running experience.

Q: Are there any waterfalls along the running trails in Sena de Luna?
A: Yes, the region features natural attractions like waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4073598" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Calderones Gorge&lt;/a&gt; area, which is explored by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052018123/ruta-de-los-calderones-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de los Calderones Trail&lt;/a&gt;, is home to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5558248" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall of the Arroyo de los Calderones&lt;/a&gt;, offering a beautiful natural spectacle for runners.

Q: Are there places to eat or drink near the running trails in Sena de Luna?
A: Sena de Luna and its surrounding villages offer local establishments where you can find refreshments or a meal after your run. While specific cafes or pubs directly on every trailhead might be limited, you'll typically find options in the main settlements like Sena de Luna itself or nearby villages, especially after completing a route.
tips: Picturesque forest track on the path from Abelgas to Mallo that runs near the Cuartero stream.
A few meters from the dirt road from Abelgas to Mallo, we find a beautiful panoramic view. Passage area of the El Cordel de Merinas route of the Babia y Luna Natural Park.

The San Lorenzo pass, located at an altitude of 1,402 meters, offers splendid views of the area of ''La Sierra'' and is the highest point of the Cordel de Merinas route that starts from Abelgas de Luna.
Nice path between oaks and brooms that runs through the Cordel de Merinas. An ideal section for the hottest months.

faqs.question: Where does the Cordel de Merinas Trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The Cordel de Merinas Trail is a circular route that starts and ends in the village of Abelgas de Luna, located within the Valles de Omaña y Luna Biosphere Reserve. You can typically find parking within the village itself, near the trailhead.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for jogging on this trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. It begins by winding through Abelgas de Luna village, then follows the Cuartero stream through a deep limestone gorge. You'll also encounter pastures with karst formations like sinkholes. While some sections have gentle slopes and soft surfaces, others can be 'technical' with pronounced descents. Be aware that parts can be slick with leaves and rocks, especially after rain, so careful footing is essential.

faqs.question: Is the Cordel de Merinas Trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its 'moderate' difficulty for jogging and the presence of varied, sometimes technical terrain with pronounced descents, this trail is generally more suitable for experienced trail runners or joggers comfortable with uneven surfaces. Beginners might find certain sections challenging, particularly when wet.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience spectacular landscapes within the Biosphere Reserve. Highlights include the Path from Abelgas to Mallo, near the Cuartero stream, offering views of a deep limestone gorge. You'll also pass by the View of Prado Espino, the Collado de San Lorenzo, and a beautiful Path Through Oak Trees. The route also boasts views of the Pereda River with several waterfalls.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit for jogging?
faqs.answer: The area is particularly vibrant and beautiful in spring, making it an excellent time to visit. However, be mindful that during wet seasons, the path can become slick due to leaves and rocks, requiring extra caution. Autumn also offers stunning scenery with changing foliage.

faqs.question: Is the Cordel de Merinas Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail is generally accessible, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially given its location within the Valles de Omaña y Luna Biosphere Reserve. This helps protect local wildlife and ensures a pleasant experience for all trail users.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the Biosphere Reserve?
faqs.answer: As the trail is located within the Valles de Omaña y Luna Biosphere Reserve, there are typically no specific permits or entrance fees required for general access to the trail itself. However, always respect local regulations and signage regarding protected areas.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities or cafes nearby after a run?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in Abelgas de Luna. While it's a small village, you might find local establishments or services. For a wider range of amenities, including cafes and pubs, you may need to venture to larger towns in the surrounding Valles de Omaña y Luna region.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a run on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the varied and potentially technical terrain, sturdy trail running shoes with good grip are highly recommended. Bring enough water, especially on warmer days, as sources may be limited. Depending on the season, consider layers of clothing, and a light rain jacket if there's a chance of wet weather, as parts of the trail can become slick.

faqs.question: Are there any specific challenges or insider tips for this route?
faqs.answer: Be prepared for a pronounced descent and potentially technical sections, especially if the path is wet from leaves and rocks. While the route is circular, some experienced runners suggest tackling it in a specific direction to manage the descents more comfortably. Always pay attention to your footing, particularly along the gorge and karst formations.

waypoints.name: Path from Abelgas to Mallo, near the Cuartero stream
